resources.string: Hides active VPN connection from selected apps — NetworkCapabilities, NetworkInfo, NetworkInterface, LinkProperties, /proc/net

Install vpnhide-ports.zip via KernelSU-Next or Magisk to block selected apps from reaching localhost ports. This hides locally-bound VPN/proxy daemons from apps that probe them via connect(127.0.0.1, PORT).
Tap P to mark an app as a proxy-hide observer. Observer apps receive ECONNREFUSED when they try to connect to any port on 127.0.0.1 or ::1 — indistinguishable from a real closed port.
Safe for banks, госуслуги, marketplaces, non-browser apps. Chromium-based browsers use localhost for dev/PWA features — avoid adding them as observers.
Rules are re-applied on every Save. After reboot, the module restores them automatically via service.sh.
Tap L, K, Z to toggle individual layers (LSPosed, Kernel module, Zygisk). Tap the row to toggle all layers at once.
Some apps detect Zygisk hooks. For those apps, keep Z disabled and rely on other protection layers.
Mark apps as H (Hidden) to remove them from the package manager for O (Observer) apps. Observers cannot list, resolve, or query any Hidden app.
System callers (installer, launcher, system services) are always exempt so core OS functionality keeps working.
Changes take effect immediately, but observer apps may cache the package list — force-stop and restart them to see results.
VPN Hide needs root access to manage target apps. Grant root permissions to this app in your root manager (Magisk, KernelSU, etc.) and reopen the app.

Off by default — VPN Hide keeps logcat near-silent. You don\'t need to flip this to collect a bug report: Start recording and Collect debug log above automatically turn logging on for the duration of the capture. Use this toggle only if you want logs to be emitted continuously. Zygisk-hooked apps must be restarted for the change to take effect for them.
Records unfiltered logs from all buffers (main/system/crash/events/radio) for debugging issues that aren\'t visible in the standard checks. Start recording, reproduce the bug, then stop.
No active VPN detected. Turn on your VPN connection before running checks.
VPN is active. This app is in the target list. Ready to run checks.
Added this app to the target list. Restart the app for native-level checks to take effect.
Adding this app to target list…
Network access is disabled for this app. ioctl checks (SIOCGIFFLAGS, SIOCGIFMTU, SIOCGIFCONF) cannot run without it. Enable in Settings → Apps → VPN Hide → Mobile data & Wi-Fi.
App was just added to targets. Restart the app to check protection.
No active VPN. Turn on VPN to check protection status.
Kernel module is not supported for your device. Install %1$s.
Some apps can detect Zygisk hooks. For those apps, disable Zygisk protection and use other protection methods instead.
No native module installed. Install kmod or zygisk for full protection.
LSPosed/Vector is not installed. Install it and enable the VPN Hide module for System Framework.
LSPosed is configured correctly, but its hooks are not active yet. Reboot the device to activate them.
Failed to read LSPosed/Vector configuration. Reopen LSPosed/Vector, verify that VPN Hide is enabled there, and then reopen VPN Hide.
VPN Hide is present in LSPosed/Vector, but the module is disabled. Enable VPN Hide in LSPosed/Vector and keep System Framework in its scope.
VPN Hide is enabled in LSPosed/Vector, but System Framework is missing from its scope. Add System Framework to the VPN Hide scope in LSPosed/Vector.
VPN Hide should stay scoped only to System Framework in LSPosed/Vector. Remove these apps from the VPN Hide scope: %1$s. Use Protection → Tun in VPN Hide for filtering instead.
No target apps configured. Go to Protection → Tun to select apps.
Ports hiding module is installed but no observers are configured. Go to Protection → Ports to pick apps.
LSPosed module version mismatch: running %1$s, installed %2$s. Reboot to apply the update.
Kernel module version %1$s is older than app version %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update.
Zygisk version %1$s is older than app version %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update.
Kernel module version %1$s is newer than app version %2$s. Update the VPN Hide app.
Zygisk version %1$s is newer than app version %2$s. Update the VPN Hide app.
Kernel module version mismatch: module %1$s, app %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update the module.
Zygisk version mismatch: module %1$s, app %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update the module.
Ports module version %1$s is older than app version %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update.
Ports module version %1$s is newer than app version %2$s. Update the VPN Hide app.
Ports module version mismatch: module %1$s, app %2$s. Open KernelSU/Magisk → Modules to update the module.
